title: Dockerfile Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Dockerfile commands and patterns for building Docker images.

commands.command:
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    python3 \
    python3-pip

commands.description: Use .dockerignore to exclude files from the image

commands.command:
FROM node:14 AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN npm install

FROM node:14
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app .
commands.description: Leverage multi-stage builds to reduce image size
commands.scenario: Leverage multi-stage builds to reduce image size

commands.description: Uses BuildKit cache mounts to persist package manager caches between builds, significantly speeding up subsequent builds.
Defines a command to check the container's health status, allowing the Docker engine to restart unhealthy containers.
Defines variables that users can pass at build-time using --build-arg, allowing for dynamic image customization.
Sets the user for subsequent instructions and the runtime, following the principle of least privilege to avoid running as root.
Adds metadata to the image, such as versioning, authorship, or description, which can be queried via 'docker inspect'.
